title: Java PEM Encoding
description: Encode and decode cryptographic keys as PEM text.
learningGoals: Encode a public key to PEM text with PEMEncoder.of().encodeToString.
Decode PEM text back into a typed key with PEMDecoder.
Note: the PEM encoding API is a preview feature in Java 26, enabled with --enable-preview.

completionChecks.prompt: What representation does PEMEncoder produce in this lesson?
choices: A ZIP archive of the key pair
A PEM text representation of the public key
completionChecks.explanation: PEM encoding serializes cryptographic objects into textual PEM form for storage or exchange.

completionChecks.prompt: What type does decoder.decode(pem, PublicKey.class) request?
choices: A PublicKey restored from PEM text
A BufferedReader over the PEM file
completionChecks.explanation: The decoder call asks for a PublicKey result, reversing the public-key PEM text produced by the encoder.

completionChecks.prompt: Which status should learners remember for this API in Java 26?
choices: It is a preview PEM API and needs preview treatment
It is removed with the Applet API
It is an automatic module feature only
It is a Java 8 final API
completionChecks.explanation: The lesson header and goals mark the PEM encoding API as preview, so it is not Java SE 25 exam-ready content.
